briefing.opening: Hello everyone. Today, May 7, 2026, as a climate change expert spokesperson and science journalist, I will begin today's climate briefing.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Global Climate Crisis and Increased Vulnerability
sections.content: According to news reported today, extreme droughts due to climate change are intensifying globally [53], and warnings have emerged that most of South America's cloud forests could disappear [48]. In Africa, countries are experiencing the dual hardship of simultaneous floods and droughts [3], and a study indicating that malaria cases could increase by 123 million by 2050 demonstrates the severe threat climate change poses to human health [73]. Both domestically and internationally, climate change is causing new patterns of population movement, raising concerns about whether South Korea is adequately prepared for an era of climate refugees [5, 6, 7, 9]. Specifically, an analysis of authoritarian regimes failing in disaster response also mentioned North Korea's vulnerability to climate change disaster response [1].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerated Domestic Efforts Towards Carbon Neutrality and Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: Domestically, various efforts are underway to achieve carbon neutrality goals. Gosaek-dong in Suwon is leading carbon neutrality by implementing a climate change response model [2], and Gwangmyeong City is accelerating its transition to a 2050 carbon-neutral, energy-independent city through the expansion of green buildings [16, 22, 25, 26]. The National Agency for Administrative City Construction (NAACC) launched an expert advisory group to lead 2040 carbon neutrality [17], and KB Financial Group is pursuing portfolio transformation for carbon neutrality [18]. Furthermore, the legal framework for agri-photovoltaics has been established, opening the way to simultaneously achieve the dual goals of increasing farm household income and carbon neutrality [20, 37], and the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) has confirmed that a combination of solar, wind, and batteries can supply 24-hour power more cheaply than fossil fuels [44].

sections.title: ⚠️ Challenges and Policy Tasks in the Energy Transition Process
sections.content: The transition process towards carbon neutrality faces several challenges. Warnings have emerged from domestic industries that they are struggling to achieve carbon neutrality goals due to soaring energy prices [31], and an analysis of pledges by mayors and provincial governors revealed that renewable energy pledges were merely declarative, with no decarbonization pledges, highlighting a lack of political will [34]. In Europe, the 'renewable energy paradox' phenomenon, where electricity prices turn negative due to the expansion of renewable energy, is even occurring [36], and realistic difficulties in the renewable energy transition are being highlighted, such as doubts raised about Microsoft's goal of 100% renewable energy by 2030 [41]. In response, climate and environmental organizations are advocating for mandatory renewable energy use in AI data centers [42], and discussions have taken place in the National Assembly to expand energy transition to the public sector [39].

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Research and Adaptation Efforts
sections.content: In the international community, various research and efforts are underway to understand and adapt to the impacts of climate change. Research has been conducted to statistically analyze records of anomalous climate events in ancient East Asia and reconstruct ancient territories [13], and in California's Sierra Nevada region, water security is being ensured and preparations for climate change are being made through a meadow restoration project [52]. The IPCC has begun reviewing the second draft of its special report on climate change and cities, preparing to provide crucial information to policymakers [60]. Furthermore, adaptation efforts utilizing technology are noteworthy, such as a Zambian student building a machine learning system to help African farmers adapt to climate change [69], and the Legacy Crop Improvement Centre developing climate-smart maize seeds [70]. Meanwhile, an analysis showing that the UK was able to save £1.7 billion worth of gas imports thanks to wind and solar power after the Iran war once again highlights the importance of renewable energy [77].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has shown that efforts towards carbon neutrality and renewable energy transition are continuing both domestically and internationally, even amidst the deepening global climate crisis. At the same time, the challenges of the energy transition process and the urgency of policy solutions have also been clearly highlighted. This concludes today's climate briefing.

summary.phenomenon: Major oil companies are reaping substantial profits due to the Iran war.

articles.description: Bigger cars including electric can cause multiple harms, yet resistance to rise of US-style vehicles has had mixed supportOn a brisk winter’s evening in Europe’s automotive heartland, a cyclist who had pushed for safer streets went out on his bike for a final time.
